config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja demi kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak individu adalah dasar agar masyarakat tetap adil dan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh perlindungan terhadap kebebasan, martabat, dan hak dasar tiap orang. Dari sudut pandang saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang bisa menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan yang otomatis menomorsatukan kolektif di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that easily becomes an excuse for suppressing people in the name of the collective. From my perspective in China, I understand why many people emphasize social stability, public order, and shared responsibility, and I do think those matter in real life, especially during crises. But if individual rights can be overridden too easily, then society itself loses legitimacy, because people begin to feel that they are only tools for a larger goal rather than human beings with dignity. The better principle is balance: protect the common good, but set firm limits so the state or the majority cannot trample basic rights whenever it claims social benefit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling rentan justru individu atau kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Kepentingan umum memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar dan dijalankan dengan proporsional, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk menekan kebebasan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang otomatis mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya tanpa mengorbankan martabat manusia.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, it is easy to understand why people value order, stability, and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how the language of "for the greater good" can be used to silence ordinary people and ignore their dignity. Society is not truly healthy if individuals have no firm protection against the majority or the state, because then public interest becomes whatever the powerful say it is. The right balance is to respect social needs in cases like public health or safety, but never treat basic rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling mudah dirugikan biasanya justru orang kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan sosial memang penting untuk kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu hanya sah kalau dibatasi oleh hak dasar, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dianggap bisa dikorbankan kapan saja atas nama kolektif, menurut saya itu bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan masyarakat yang rawan sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights in any broad or automatic way. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize order and collective welfare, but I also know how easily “the common good” can become a vague excuse for ignoring ordinary people’s dignity and voice. A society is not truly strong if the individual has no real protection, because then public interest is defined only by those in power. I would accept limited restrictions for public health or safety, but only when they are necessary, proportionate, and never allowed to erase basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
